title: Hash 1-2 values using scratch space — no free pointer update needed
trigger_patterns: keccak256 of one or two values
solidity_before:
bytes32 h = keccak256(abi.encode(v0));
bytes32 h2 = keccak256(abi.encode(v0, v1));
explanation: abi.encode allocates new memory and updates the free pointer. For 1-2 values that fit in 64 bytes, writing directly to scratch space (0x00-0x3f) avoids all allocation overhead. The free pointer at 0x40 is never touched. For 3+ values, free pointer must be used since inputs exceed scratch space.
when_to_apply: Any keccak256 of 1-2 fixed-size values where scratch space is not already in use
when_not_to_apply: 3+ values (exceeds scratch space), when scratch space is already used by outer assembly block
